pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json metadata field and setup_task.sh line 30: Screenshot must be saved to this location
Confirmed in task.json metadata field and setup_task.sh line 31: Text file must be saved to this location

American Heart Association and clinical guidelines define normal systolic <120, Stage 1 HTN as 130-139, Stage 2 HTN as ≥140, and hypertensive crisis as ≥180. 80 mmHg represents lower range of clinically monitored values (mild hypotension). ISO 81060-validated NIBP devices measure ranges from 60-230 mmHg systolic; 80 is a conservative lower bound for clinical significance.

Confirmed by American Heart Association, Mayo Clinic, and NHLBI: 180/120 mmHg defines hypertensive crisis threshold requiring emergency care. CDC and ACC/AHA 2025 guidelines confirm 180 systolic as the emergency intervention threshold.

ISO 81060-validated NIBP monitors (e.g., HUAWEI WATCH) specify measurement ranges of 40-160 mmHg diastolic. 40 mmHg represents severe hypotension and the lower limit of typical NIBP device capabilities. This aligns with device specifications.
Confirmed by American Heart Association, Mayo Clinic, NHLBI, and ACC/AHA guidelines: 180/120 mmHg defines the hypertensive crisis threshold. 120 diastolic is the emergency intervention trigger point.

Task description references 'simulated device adapter of type NIBP' and OpenICE Supervisor. Setup scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) confirm OpenICE log analysis. OpenICE is open-source medical device interoperability platform by MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital Harvard Medical School.

pi_items.verified_value: Verifier uses different ranges: systolic 60-250 mmHg, diastolic 30-150 mmHg
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 104-105: 'if 60 < systolic < 250 and 30 < diastolic < 150'. Metadata ranges are more restrictive than verifier acceptance ranges.

privileged_info_summary: All blood pressure ranges in task metadata are verified against authoritative medical sources and clinical standards. Systolic minimum (80 mmHg) and maximum (180 mmHg) align with clinical thresholds: 80 represents mild hypotension boundary, while 180 mmHg is the hypertensive crisis emergency threshold confirmed by American Heart Association and CDC. Diastolic minimum (40 mmHg) matches ISO 81060-certified NIBP device specifications, while maximum (120 mmHg) is the emergency threshold threshold. Expected deliverables are evidence screenshot and text file containing NIBP readings in format 'Systolic: X mmHg, Diastolic: Y mmHg'. The task uses OpenICE, a simulator-based medical device interoperability platform, generating synthetic patient data rather than real clinical data. Note: Verifier logic uses broader ranges (systolic 60-250, diastolic 30-150) than metadata specification, allowing physiologically wider variance in accepted values.
